perturbation.original_text: In all instances of a conflict, between the provisions of this Agreement and the specific provisions set forth in a Statement of Work, the provisions of this Agreement shall control.
perturbation.changed_text: In most instances of a conflict, between the provisions of this Agreement and the specific provisions set forth in a Statement of Work, the provisions of this Agreement shall control, except when the Statement of Work explicitly states otherwise, in which case the Statement of Work provisions will prevail.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'except when the Statement of Work explicitly states otherwise, in which case the Statement of Work provisions will prevail,' we've created a contradiction. Section 1(a) now states that the MSA generally controls over SOWs, but the added exception allows SOWs to override the MSA. This contradicts the original unqualified statement. The original text's emphasis on the Agreement's control is now undermined, leading to uncertainty about which document governs in case of conflicting terms. This creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Company agrees to retain Contractor to perform the consulting services for Company, on a task by task basis (the "Services"), and Contractor agrees to furnish the Services on the terms and subject to the conditions set forth in this Agreement. During the term of this Agreement, Company and Contractor will develop and agree upon statements of work defining the Services and work product to be provided by Contractor, Contractor's compensation, additional terms and conditions, if any, applicable to a particular engagement and such other details as the parties deem appropriate (each a "Statement of Work"). A Statement of Work may provide specifications for deliverables to be provided thereunder (the "Specifications"). Statements of Work that are from time to time agreed upon shall reference this Agreement, and shall be executed by the parties and attached hereto and shall form a part hereof. In most instances of a conflict, between the provisions of this Agreement and the specific provisions set forth in a Statement of Work, the provisions of this Agreement shall control, except when the Statement of Work explicitly states otherwise, in which case the Statement of Work provisions will prevail.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clear contradiction by stating that the Statement of Work (SOW) can override the Agreement under certain circumstances, while the original text implied that the Agreement always controls in case of conflict. The addition of 'except when the Statement of Work explicitly states otherwise, in which case the Statement of Work provisions will prevail' directly contradicts the initial assertion that the Agreement provisions shall always control, creating ambiguity and uncertainty about which document governs in case of conflicting terms, fulfilling the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Company acknowledges that any change in Specifications may result in changes to estimated fees and estimated timeline for creation of deliverables. Company also may request a change in the Schedule without changing the scope of the Services relating to the applicable Statement of Work. In either case, the parties shall negotiate in good faith a reasonable and equitable adjustment in the applicable fees, Schedule and Specifications. Contractor shall continue work pursuant to the existing Statement of Work, and shall not be bound by any change requested by Company, until such change has been accepted in writing by Contractor.
perturbation.changed_text: Company acknowledges that any change in Specifications may result in changes to estimated fees and estimated timeline for creation of deliverables. Company also may request a change in the Schedule without changing the scope of the Services relating to the applicable Statement of Work. In either case, the parties shall negotiate in good faith a reasonable and equitable adjustment in the applicable fees, Schedule and Specifications. Contractor shall endeavor to continue work pursuant to the existing Statement of Work, and shall not be bound by any change requested by Company, until such change has been accepted in writing by Contractor.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Contractor shall continue work' with 'Contractor shall endeavor to continue work' weakens the obligation. The original text suggests an obligation to continue working under the existing SOW until changes are formally accepted. The changed text makes it merely an effort, not an obligation. This creates an in-text contradiction, especially if other sections refer to the continuation of work as a requirement. This change creates uncertainty in enforcement by allowing the Contractor to potentially pause work if changes are requested but not yet formally accepted, contradicting the implied expectation of continued performance.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Company may request changes that affect the scope or duration of the Services relating to any Statement of Work, including changes in the Specifications and changes in the deliverables to be delivered. Company acknowledges that any change in Specifications may result in changes to estimated fees and estimated timeline for creation of deliverables. Company also may request a change in the Schedule without changing the scope of the Services relating to the applicable Statement of Work. In either case, the parties shall negotiate in good faith a reasonable and equitable adjustment in the applicable fees, Schedule and Specifications. Contractor shall endeavor to continue work pursuant to the existing Statement of Work, and shall not be bound by any change requested by Company, until such change has been accepted in writing by Contractor.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Contractor shall continue work' with 'Contractor shall endeavor to continue work.' This weakens the obligation to continue working under the existing Statement of Work (SOW) while changes are being negotiated. This introduces ambiguity where the contradicted text implies a requirement for continued performance, creating an in-text contradiction. By changing the obligation from 'shall' to 'endeavor to,' the contractor is not obligated to continue work, which directly conflicts with the implication of continued performance during negotiation, making enforcement unclear.

perturbation.original_text: Contractor shall not use or disclose to any person, firm or entity any Confidential Information without Company's express, prior written permission; provided, however, that notwithstanding the foregoing, Contractor may disclose Confidential Information to the extent that it is required to be disclosed pursuant to a statutory or regulatory provision or court order.
perturbation.changed_text: Contractor shall not use or disclose to any person, firm or entity any Confidential Information without Company's express, prior written permission; provided, however, that notwithstanding the foregoing, Contractor may disclose Confidential Information to the extent that it deems necessary, in its sole discretion, for business development purposes, or as required to be disclosed pursuant to a statutory or regulatory provision or court order.
perturbation.explanation: Adding 'Contractor may disclose Confidential Information to the extent that it deems necessary, in its sole discretion, for business development purposes' introduces a broad exception to the confidentiality obligation. Section 8 originally prohibits any disclosure without express permission, except for legal requirements. The added clause allows Contractor to disclose Confidential Information for business development based on its own judgment. This creates a conflict because the definition of 'Confidential Information' remains broad, and the exception undermines the original purpose of protecting that information. The phrase 'sole discretion' directly contradicts the need for 'express, prior written permission'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information" means all documents, software, reports, data, records, forms, conversations and other materials obtained by Contractor from Company in the course of performing any Services (including, but not limited to, Company records and information). Notwithstanding the foregoing, Confidential Information does not include information which: (i) is or becomes publicly known through no wrongful act of Contractor; or (ii) is independently developed by Contractor without benefit of Company's Page 7 of 10 Confidential Information. Contractor shall not use or disclose to any person, firm or entity any Confidential Information without Company's express, prior written permission; provided, however, that notwithstanding the foregoing, Contractor may disclose Confidential Information to the extent that it deems necessary, in its sole discretion, for business development purposes, or as required to be disclosed pursuant to a statutory or regulatory provision or court order.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception allowing the Contractor to disclose Confidential Information for business development purposes at its sole discretion, directly contradicting the original requirement for 'express, prior written permission' from the Company for any disclosure, as stated in both the original and contradicted text. This introduces a clear inconsistency and undermines the protection of Confidential Information. The 'sole discretion' aspect exacerbates the contradiction, making it a strong, obvious conflict.
